title: Thelypteris lebeufii
intro: Thelypteris lebeufii, also known as Lebeuf's fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris lebeufii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
